_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
specialty: General Surgery, Pediatric Surgery, Genitourinary Surgery
clinical_indications: Persistent hemiscrotal swelling that is usually painless but may give a dragging sensation depending upon size
A feeling of heaviness in the scrotum
Increased pressure at the bottom of the penis
Pain in the groin or lower back and abdomen
Surgical removal of large or painful epididymal cysts from the scrotum

clinical_thresholds.note: Excision may be necessary if the cyst is large
Excision is indicated for painful cysts

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical evaluation and Scrotal ultrasound/FNAC confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure
Diagnosis not confirmed by clinical evaluation or Scrotal ultrasound/FNAC
Cyst not documented as large or painful to justify surgical excision
Scrotal ultrasound not submitted at pre-authorization
Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details missing or incomplete
Detailed procedure / Operative Notes not available
Discharge summary with follow-up advice not provided

clinical_key_pointers: An epididymal cyst is a fluid-filled, thin-walled cysts found posterior to the testis, usually in the upper or lower pole of the epididymis. They are very common, usually multiple and vary in size at presentation.
Aetiology: Cystic degeneration of the appendages of epididymis—congenital
Site: Behind and above the testis in the region of epididymal head
Aspiration: Results in recurrence as the cyst is multilocular
Excision: Excision may be necessary if the cyst is large
surgical_options: Surgical removal – Epididymal cyst removal or excision is a procedure to remove large or painful cysts from the scrotum
